title: hieracium leiocephalum subsp. abortivum
intro: Hieracium leiocephalum subsp. abortivum, a lesser-known species within the Hawkweed genus, is a small perennial plant characterized by its delicate leaves and small, yellow dandelion-like flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium leiocephalum subsp. abortivum are said to include wound healing, the reduction of inflammation, and the prevention of infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
